# Editorial Commander Morning Brief

**Date:** 2026-06-29
**Repository commit:** 4db0aab
**Branch:** main
**Generated by:** Editorial Commander V1.2

> **V1.2 NOTE — Production Detection Fix Applied**
> This brief corrects a critical error in V1.1. Files under `content/articles/` are **source articles**, NOT published pages. Under V1.1, all 38 source articles were incorrectly counted as "Already Covered." Under V1.2, they are classified as **READY TO PUBLISH** — completed work that has not yet been deployed to root. This dramatically changes the coverage picture and the recommended action.

---

## Repository Health

**Overall status: 🔴 RED**

**Reason for RED:** 20 completed source articles are awaiting deployment to root. The repository appears to have significantly less published content than it actually has — 20 finished articles exist in source form only. This is a production pipeline blockage, not a content gap.

### Published Pages

- **21 informational articles published at root** — 5 portal guides + 16 standalone informational pages
- **29 product/brand pages published at root**
- **62 total published pages**

### Source Articles (NOT published)

- **20 source articles are READY TO PUBLISH** — completed, gap-report-targeted, never deployed
- **18 source articles are undocumented** — no gap report targets them (pre-gap-report content)
- These 38 files exist in `content/articles/` and are invisible to search engines at their current location

### Duplicate Topic Candidates

Under V1.2 rules (root only), the previous "duplicate pairs" from V1.1 disappear — the articles-only files are not published, so there is no published duplicate. However:
- `/multivitamin-kvinnor-over-40.html` (root, published) partially overlaps in topic with `content/articles/multivitamin-for-kvinnor.html` (source, broader scope). Publishing the source article alongside the root 40+ article creates two semantically adjacent published pages. Recommend publishing and adding clear differentiation / cross-linking.

### Missing Authority Research

11 domains missing (same as V1.1) — kreatin, adaptogener, longevity, PMS/hormoner, melatonin, kollagen, timingguide, protein, hud/hår/naglar, järnbrist, kosttillskott-general-safety.

### Coverage Conflicts

- `kosttillskott-gap.md` Cluster 12 (D-vitamin dosering): source article exists in `content/articles/d-vitamin-dosering-per-dag.html` — same as global-batch #7. When published, this is covered by one deployment.
- `production-status.md` claims 25/25 generated (100%) — this is correct for source file generation but does not reflect root publication status.

### Undocumented Root Articles

The following 21 published root informational articles do not appear as targets in any gap report (they predate the gap report system):
- ala-vs-epa-vs-dha, direktforsaljning-fakta, ekologisk-stadning-greenwashing, finns-det-billigare-alternativ, fytosteroler-cellmembran, hur-fungerar-natverksmarknadsforing-egentligen, karotenoid-tillskott-vs-mat, magnesiumglycinat-och-somn, multivitamin-kvinnor-over-40, vad-ar-kostfiber, vad-ar-probiotika, vad-ar-vitamin-b12, varfor-fiskolja-inte-ar-likvardigt, vaxtbaserade-steroler-dagligen, viktuppgang-klimakteriet, zeaxantin-immunforsvar-2025

### Production Blockers

- **No blocker for READY TO PUBLISH items** — 20 source articles are complete and can be deployed immediately.
- **Kosttillskott A-priority items blocked** by missing authority research (11 domains).

---

## Drift Detection

| # | Type | Finding |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **READY TO PUBLISH × 20** | 20 source articles in `content/articles/` have no corresponding root HTML page. These are the highest-priority operational items. They represent the completed second half of the global-batch-2026-06-29 backlog. |
| 2 | GAP TOPIC COVERED | global-batch clusters #1–5 (portal guides) are correctly published at root. ✅ |
| 3 | PRODUCTION STATUS INACCURATE | `production-status.md` marks 25/25 as "Generated" which means source files were created — but does NOT track whether they were published to root. The 20 missing root pages represent a deployment gap the status file does not capture. |
| 4 | UNDOCUMENTED SOURCE ARTICLES × 18 | 18 `content/articles/` files have no gap report target: ar-miljovanliga-rengoringsmedel-lika-effektiva, cellmembran-funktion, c-vitamin-tillskott-vs-serum-huden, forsknings-faq, hudtecken-naringsbrist, levnytt-principer, lutein-zeaxantin-huden, naringsbrist, naringsbrist-symptom, nya-kostrad-65-plus-d-vitamin-magnesium, retinol-pa-sommaren, vad-ar-niacinamid, vad-ar-vaxtsteroler, var-metod, varfor-ar-jag-trott-hela-tiden. These predate the gap report system. |
| 5 | NEW GAP REPORTS NOT IN PRODUCTION STATUS | `mlm-gap.md`, `kosttillskott-gap.md`, `omega-3-gap.md` are not reflected in `production-status.md`. |

## Recommended Action

**Action:** Publish Source Article
**Target:** Magnesiumformer — vilken ska man välja? (highest-scored READY TO PUBLISH item)
**Slug:** `/magnesiumformer-vilken-valja`
**Source:** `global-batch-2026-06-29-gap.md` (#6), `magnesium-gap.md` (Cluster 2)
**Source file:** `content/articles/magnesiumformer-vilken-valja.html`
**Authority:** CLEARED — article is written and in source; no new research needed

**Rationale:**

The single highest-value action is to begin deploying the 20 completed source articles that are invisible to users and search engines in their current `content/articles/` location. Magnesiumformer (88/100) is the highest-scored article in the READY TO PUBLISH queue. It requires zero new writing — the source file exists, was generated from the highest-priority gap report, and simply needs to be copied/moved to the root directory and served as a published page.

Publishing all 20 READY TO PUBLISH articles in sequence (by score order) would instantly increase LevNytt's published informational article count from 21 to 41 — nearly doubling the site's content surface — without any new research or production work. This should be completed before any new article writing begins, as it delivers the highest output-per-effort ratio in the entire pipeline.

After all 20 READY TO PUBLISH articles are deployed, the next action is to write the first new MLM article (MLM-produkter och kosttillskott, 90/100) followed by the Omega-3 portal guide.

---

## Warnings

1. **CRITICAL — 20 articles not deployed:** The global-batch-2026-06-29 backlog generated 25 articles but only 5 portal guides were deployed to root. The remaining 20 are invisible source files. This must be the next operational priority.

2. **production-status.md must be updated:** The status file tracks "Generated" (source file creation) but not "Published" (root deployment). A new "Published" column or a separate deployment-status.md should be added when the deployment pipeline is clarified.

3. **Deployment workflow is undefined:** It is unclear whether `content/articles/` files require a build step, CMS deployment, or manual copy to become root pages. This workflow must be established before the 20 articles can be deployed. If this is a manual copy operation, it can be done immediately.

4. **QA debt — 3 Session 1 portal guides missing GSV/Pinterest meta:** `multivitamin-komplett-guide.html`, `magnesium-komplett-guide.html`, `probiotika-komplett-guide.html` are the three highest-traffic portals and are missing social metadata.

5. **18 undocumented source articles need a gap report:** The beauty/skin cluster (retinol, niacinamid, C-vitamin serum, lutein), the ecology article, naringsbrist cluster, and several foundational articles predate the gap report system. Running a beauty/skin or general catch-up gap report would document these.

---

## Summary

The V1.2 analysis reveals a critical production pipeline gap that V1.1 obscured: 20 completed informational articles exist in `content/articles/` but have never been deployed to root. Under V1.1's incorrect classification, these were counted as "Already Covered," hiding the deployment backlog entirely. The repository's published informational article count is actually 21 — not 59 — and the 5 portal guides remain the only published substantive content from the global-batch backlog.

The immediate recommendation is to deploy the 20 READY TO PUBLISH source articles to root, starting with Magnesiumformer (88/100). This requires no new writing and will nearly double the site's published content surface in a single operational step. Once the deployment pipeline is clarified and all 20 articles are live, the pipeline should proceed to new article production: first the MLM-produkter och kosttillskott article (90/100, fully authority-cleared), then the Omega-3 portal guide, and eventually the kosttillskott cluster (once 11 authority research runs unblock it).

Repository health is RED due to the 20 undeployed articles. It will return to AMBER once deployment begins and GREEN once all 20 are live and the workflow is documented.
